LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DON BATTAGLIA AND NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS DBA S.F. SEA FOOD EXPRESS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SUSTAINED AS TO ALL CLAIMS WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MIGUEL SANCHEZ NEEDS TO CLARIFY PRECISELY THE CLAIMS HE IS ALLEGING, THE RELIEF HE IS SEEKING AND THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR SUING ON BEHALF OF OTHERS. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALSO NEEDS TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL ULTIMATE FACTS REGARDING MOST OF THE CLAIMS, BUT IN PARTICULAR THE WRONGFUL DISCHARGE, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION AND RETALIATION CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2012 Continued To Nov-14-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Colleen Coen, Esq., of Modena & Royce, Tel. 650.589.3913, for Defendant and moving party;
Rafael Crespo, Esq., of the L/O of Rafael Crespo, Tel. 415.295.2063 for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling with modification. DEFENDANTS NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS DBA S.F. and DON BATTAGLIA MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. The motion to strike is untimely. There is no stipulation by the parties to extend the time to file the motion to strike. Plaintiff only agreed to extend time for the purposes of providing an Answer to the complaint. Defendant to answer in 10 days. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR # 7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Colleen Coen, Esq., of Modena & Royce, Tel. 650.589.3913, for Defendant and moving party;
Rafael Crespo, Esq., of the L/O of Rafael Crespo, Tel. 415.295.2063 for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling with modification. DEFENDANTS DON BATTAGLIA and NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The demurrer is untimely. There is no stipulation by the parties to extend the time to file the demurrer. Plaintiff only agreed to extend time for the purposes of providing an Answer to the complaint. Defendant to answer in 10 days. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and submit it by email to the court.) Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR # 7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-02-2012 Continued To Sep-26-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. COLLEEN COEN OF MODEN & ROYCE, TEL. NO. (650) 589-3913 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DON BATTAGLIA AND NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS DBA S.F. SEA FOOD EXPRESS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained as to all claims with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Miguel Sanchez needs to clarify precisely the claims he is alleging, the relief he is seeking and the statutory basis for suing on behalf of others. The amended complaint also needs to allege additional ultimate facts regarding most of the claims, but in particular the wrongful discharge, negligent infliction and retaliation claims. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
